sections.title: Before learning how to catch Implings from Elnock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a gnome doing here?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I'm an investigator. I'm watching the implings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would you want to do that?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: My client has asked me to find out where certain missing items have been going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is this client?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I'm not at liberty to discuss that. Investigator-client confidentiality don't you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this place?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: The fairies call it Puro-Puro. It seems to be the home plane of the implings.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I don't think these creatures have a name for it. As you can see there isn't a lot else here other than wheat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you get here?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: The same way you did, I suspect. Through a portal in a wheat field. I followed one back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't noticed them do that.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: That's why I'm the investigator and you're the adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what are these implings?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: That's a very interesting question. My best guess is that they are relatives to imps, which is why there are imps here as well.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Implings do appear to like collecting objects and, as my clients have noted, have no concept of ownership. However, I do not sense any malicious intent.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: It is my observation that they collect things that other creatures want, rather than they want them themselves. It seems to provide them with sustenance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they feed off our desire for things?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Possibly. Either way, it seems that they almost exclusively collect things that people want, except their younglings who I infer haven't learnt the best things to collect yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the more experienced implings have the more desirable items?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: That is my observation. Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I catch these implings, then?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Indeed you may. In fact I encourage it. You will, however, require some equipment.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Firstly you will need a butterfly net in which to catch them and at least one special impling jar to store an impling.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: You will also require some experience as a Hunter since these creatures are elusive. The more immature implings require less experience, but some of the rarer implings are extraordinarily hard to find and catch.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Once you have caught one, you may break the jar open and obtain the object the impling is carrying. Alternatively, you may exchange certain combinations of jars with me. I will return the jars to my clients. In

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: exchange I will be able to provide you with some equipment that may help you hunt butterflies more effectively.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Also, beware. Those imps walking around the maze do not like the fact that their kindred spirits are being captured and will attempt to steal any full jars you have on you, setting the implings free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard I may find dragon equipment in Puro-Puro.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Have you indeed? Well, that may well be true, though bear in mind that implings are quite small so they are unlikely to be lugging a sizeable shield around with them. However, since it seems that dragon items are

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: very desirable to humans then it certainly is possible that the most expert implings may try to obtain such equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, it's true then? Cool!

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I should warn you, though, if the impling is strong enough to collect dragon equipment, then you will have to be very skilled at hunting implings in order to catch them.

sections.title: After learning how to catch Implings from Elnock

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Ah, good day, it's you again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Ah, I notice you don't own an impling collector's scroll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me how to catch implings again?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Certainly.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Firstly you will need a butterfly net in which to catch them and at least one special impling jar to store an impling.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: You will also require some experience as a Hunter since these creatures are elusive. The more immature implings require less experience, but some of the rarer implings are extraordinarily hard to find and catch.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Once you have caught one, you may break the jar open and obtain the object the impling is carrying. Alternatively, you may exchange certain combinations of jars with me. I will return the jars to my clients. In

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: exchange I will be able to provide you with some equipment that may help you hunt butterflies more effectively.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Also, beware. Those imps walking around the maze do not like the fact that their kindred spirits are being captured and will attempt to steal any full jars you have on you, setting the implings free.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: You cannot use an ordinary butterfly jar as a container as the implings will escape from them with ease. However, I have done some investigation and have come up with a solution - if a butterfly jar is coated

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: with a thin layer of a substance noxious to them they become incapable of escape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What substance is that, then?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I have tried a few experiments with the help of a friend back home, and it turns out that a combination of anchovy oil and flowers - marigolds, rosemary or nasturtiums - will work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you make anchovy oil then?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I'd grind up some cooked anchovies and pass them through a sieve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I make these jars?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Well, I believe there is a chemist in Rimmington that has a small still that you could use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anywhere I can buy these jars?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Well I may be able to let you have a few - if it means you will start hunting these implings - although I do not have an infinite supply.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about these thieving imps.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Imps and implings appear to be related, and the imps here are quite protective of their smaller relations. If you allow them to get too close then they will attempt to steal jarred implings from your pack, if you have them.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: They will then set them free, dropping your jar on the floor. So, if you're quick, you may be able to catch it again.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I have some impling deterrent which I may trade if you prove that you can catch implings well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's this equipment you can give me, then?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I have been given permission by my clients to give three pieces of equipment to able hunters.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Firstly, I have some imp deterrent. If you bring me three baby implings, two young implings and one gourmet impling already jarred, I will give you a vial. Imps don't like the smell, so they will be less likely to

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: steal jarred implings from you.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Secondly, I have magical butterfly nets. If you bring me three gourmet implings, two earth implings and one essence impling I will give you a new net. It will help you catch both implings and butterflies.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Lastly, I have magical jar generators. If you bring me three essence implings, two eclectic implings and one nature impling I will give you a jar generator. This object will create either butterfly or impling jars (up to

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: a limited number of charges) without having to carry a pack full of them.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Yes! I have some spare equipment for you.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: There you go. You have everything you need now.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I have already given you some equipment.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: If you are ready to start hunting implings, then enter the main part of the maze.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Just push through the wheat that surrounds the centre of the maze and get catching!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you store some equipment for me?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I suppose I could keep hold of a net and a few empty jars for you. Just give me what you want me to hold.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I have already given you some equipment.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: If you are ready to start hunting implings, then enter the main part of the maze.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Just push through the wheat that surrounds the centre of the maze and get catching!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the wheat?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: The wheat here can be hard to push through and pushing through it definitely makes you stronger. I found it easier to push through it for about half an hour just after coming through a temporary crop circle.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I call it the Farmer's Affinity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I didn't want to become stronger pushing through the wheat?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I could give you oil to rub on you to reduce friction. Still as hard to push but you don't get stronger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You gave me oil to stop me getting stronger. What if I changed my mind?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I could lend you a cloth to wipe it off. That should let you become strong while pushing through the wheat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a few impling jars?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: You've already purchased 10 jars today, that's enough. Please come back tomorrow if you'd like to buy more.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I usually prefer trading these jars for implings, but if you desperately need them I'm willing to sell you up to 10 jars per day.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: I will sell these jars for 2,000 coins each. How many would you like to purchase?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: My time is valuable, please give me a serious offer next time.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: So you would like to purchase [number] [jar/jars], costing you a total of [number] coins. Is that correct?

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: You don't seem to have brought enough coins, please come back with [number] coins if you'd like to buy the jars.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Here you go. You can come back if you need more and tomorrow I will have another 10 jars ready for purchase.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: Here you go. If you need some more I'll have 10 new jars ready for purchase tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: My time is valuable, please give me a serious offer next time.

lines.speaker: Elnock Inquisitor
lines.text: That's too many, I can let you purchase a maximum of [number] jars for a total of [number] coins today.
